calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-09-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Rueppel for and with (administrator/petitioner) Willie Johnson.

The Court stated that there are a few issues and that a procedural issue is that verification is needed. Mr. Rueppel responded that Ms. Johnson signed the verification this morning and that it is in the process of being e-filed. The Court stated that notwithstanding the agreement that the sale needed court confirmation the property was sold anyway, even though the ex parte request to do so was denied, that the court understands that there was urgency but still a court order was needed and that court orders cannot be ignored. Mr. Rueppel responded that the Davis estate was involved in the sale, that Ms. Johnson was the only one that could be harmed, that there was no prejudice to any party and that they had a duty to the estate. The Court asked if they believed it was okay to ignore the court order. Mr. Rueppel stated that they had asked the court to allow it to go through, that they were unable to get information from Judge Alvarado as to why it was denied, that it was in the best interest of the estate to go through with the sale and that they are not sure what the other options would have been. The Court stated that they could have come in with a stipulation, they could have filed a report of sale, that many things could have been done, that you cannot ignore a court order, asked what to do now and stated that maybe an outlet is responding to the fee requests. Mr. Rueppel stated that if the court is going to reduce the fees he wanted it to know that a great deal of time was spent preventing the foreclosure. The Court stated that the trustees notice of sale was never filed. Mr. Rueppel stated that it would have required further legal efforts and apologized for advising his client to go against the court order. The Court stated that the statutory fee is 3 times what ordinary services were and that there are extraordinary fees towards the sale, that work was done contrary to the court order and that otherwise the final distribution can go forward. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Ex Parte Application For Order Advancing Hearing Date
Off Calendar Feb-10-2016 Denied

Petition For Enforcement Of Mediation Agreement
Off Calendar Mar-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Rueppel for (petitioner) Willie Johnson. Brent Kato for (beneficiary) Troy Davis.

Mr. Kato stated that he has no objection to the settlement or the terms, that Mr. Davis has agreed to be the successor administrator and that he is not expecting opposition to this. Mr. Rueppel stated that he has filed a response (and presented an endorsed copy to Judge Busch), that he has been informed that the mortgage has not been paid for several months and that they will need to sell the property. The Court granted the petition for enforcement of mediation agreement (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Mar-19-2014 Continued To Apr-16-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard M. Grabstein for Janet Davis. John Rueppel for Willie Johnson.

Mr. Grabstein stated that the parties were set for mediation this afternoon at 1:00 pm. The Court continued the matter for status on mediation to April 16, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel are to notify the assigned Probate Examiner of the status of mediation by April 11, 2014. Mr. Rueppel noted that there may be more discovery.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition To Confirm Real Property And Personal Property To Estate
Off Calendar Oct-02-2013 Continued To Jan-22-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Grabstein for and with Janet Davis. John Reuppel for Willie Johnson.

Mr. Grabstein stated that Ms. Davis was here as Administrator of the Estate of John Davis (PES-13-296683) to object to Mr. Reuppel's petition, that they were willing to do mediation and that he will file a formal objection. Mr. Rueppel stated that he will discuss mediation with his Client. The Parties were informed to contact the Clerk if they are interested in participating in Pro Bono Mediation so that the Court can issue an order referring the case. The Court raised the issue of proper notice/personal service. Mr. Grabstein stated that the Davis Estate has been paying all of the mortgage and taxes on the Twine Estate. Counsel agreed to exchange information. The Petition to Confirm Real Property and Personal Property to Estate and the Petition to Determine Title to and Require Transfer of Real Property from Decedent's Estate were continued to January 22, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)
